evals.prompt: I want to build a voice agent for a pizza ordering service using Kuralle. It should use the native audio pipeline with Gemini so it responds fast. Show me a complete working server setup.
evals.expected_output: A complete server using createVoiceSession({ mode: 'realtime', options: { runtime: createRuntime(...) } }) (not cascaded pipeline), binding a WebSocketAgentServer (or LiveKit server.onConnection), using a Gemini RealtimeModel, with createRuntime wiring in the defineAgent, and a clean graceful shutdown. Should NOT use KuralleVoiceSession or TurnDetector (those are cascaded, not native).
expectations: Uses createVoiceSession with mode realtime or createKuralleRealtimeAgent with runtime — not KuralleVoiceSession
Uses a Gemini model (gemini-2.5-flash-preview or similar) — not a cascaded STT/TTS stack
Includes a WebSocketAgentServer or server.onConnection listener to accept connections
Does NOT incorrectly recommend TurnDetector (cascaded-only feature)
Includes graceful shutdown (SIGTERM/SIGINT handling or equivalent)

evals.prompt: My Kuralle voice agent is working over WebSocket but I need to also accept calls via SIP (for a 3CX PBX). How do I add SIP transport support?
evals.expected_output: Instructions and code using @kuralle-agents/livekit-plugin-transport-sip (SIPAgentServer), showing the transport config with the correct sampleRate (24000), and how to point a 3CX trunk at the agent endpoint. Should mention the SIP client configuration requirements (PCMU/PCMA codec, G.711 at 8kHz for SIP transcoding vs 24kHz for the pipeline).
expectations: Uses SIPAgentServer (not WebSocketAgentServer) for SIP connections
Explains the codec chain: SIP sends G.711 at 8kHz, the transport resamples to 24kHz for the native audio model — the developer does NOT need to configure sampleRate manually
Provides guidance on pointing a SIP trunk (3CX or generic) to the agent endpoint
Does NOT confuse SIP transport with the WebSocket transport

evals.prompt: I'm building a voice agent that collects appointment info across multiple turns — first the caller's name, then their preferred date. How do I set up extraction nodes so it transitions automatically when both are collected? Also, I know voice models can hallucinate extracted slots, how do I prevent that?
evals.expected_output: A defineAgent with a collect node gathering name + date, required fields set, onComplete pointing to the next node, and critically: extractionModel set on createRuntime (not just on the agent). Should also show .voiceRules() or equivalent voice prompt guidance. Should NOT have the user manually write a submit tool (collect nodes handle multi-turn gather internally).
expectations: Uses collect node kind for the data-collection step
Sets required with both fields on the collect node
Sets onComplete to auto-advance when fields are collected
Sets extractionModel on createRuntime — not just on the agent config
Does NOT add a manual submit tool (would conflict with collect loop)
Mentions or applies voice prompt rules (short utterances, one ask per turn)
